briefs.title: Council approved five new non-residential zoning districts for Burnaby 2050
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved five new non-residential zoning districts as part of the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan. The changes update the Zoning Bylaw to match the city's long-term land use framework. A public hearing was not held because the amendments are consistent with the already-approved community plan. The City Solicitor has been authorized to finalize the bylaw amendments.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property's zoning is affected by the new non-residential districts and review the updated Burnaby Zoning Bylaw.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
7.1 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS - ZONING BYLAW REWRITE PROJECT - REVISED NEW NON-RESIDENTIAL ZONING DISTRICTS

The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report proposing revised amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to establish five new zoning districts and related changes to implement the Burnaby 2050 OCP land use framework.
*Councillor Keithley left at 5:42 pm and returned at 5:45 pm
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 057

THAT the proposed amendments to Burnaby Zoning Bylaw, 1965, as described in Section 3.0 of the report titled “Proposed Bylaw Amendments – Zoning Bylaw Rewrite – Revised New Non-Residential Zoning Districts”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved;

THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to Burnaby Zoning Bylaw, 1965, substantially as set out in Attachment 1 and Attachment 2 of the report; and

THAT a Public Hearing not be held for the proposed amendments to Burnaby Zoning Bylaw, 1965 described in the report, as they are consistent with the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved removing restaurant-only restriction on Fraser Reach Court property
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to authorize the discharge of a Section 219 restrictive covenant on a property at 8340 Fraser Reach Court. A Section 219 covenant is a legal agreement registered on the property title that limits how the land can be used. In this case, the covenant had restricted a portion of the property to restaurant use only. Council's decision removes that restriction, giving the property owner more flexibility in how they can use or develop that part of the site.
briefs.action_required: If you own or are considering purchasing 8340 Fraser Reach Court, contact the City's planning department to confirm the covenant discharge has been registered on title.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
7.2 PROPOSED COVENANT DISCHARGE AT 8340 FRASER REACH COURT (REZ #14-31)

The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking Council authorization to discharge a Section 219 restrictive covenant on title, which restricts the use of a portion of the subject property for restaurant use only.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 058

THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to discharge the existing Section 219 Covenant restricting uses on a portion of the site at 8340 Fraser Reach Court for restaurant use only.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council authorized amendments to Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a resolution authorizing the City Solicitor to bring forward amendments to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 (Bylaw No. 14610). The changes, outlined in a February 10, 2026 report, will update the rules for how council meetings and procedures are run. This decision is final and the amendments will now be drafted and presented to council for adoption.
briefs.source_body:
7.3 BURNABY PROCEDURE BYLAW UPDATE

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report seeking Council authorization to bring forward amendments to Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 059
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward the amendments to Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024, Bylaw No. 14610, as outlined in the report titled “Burnaby Procedure Bylaw Update”, dated February 10, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council referred Lochdale School Park playground report back to staff
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to refer a report on the Lochdale School Park playground replacement back to staff for more work. The original recommendation was not moved. This means no decision has been made on the playground's future, and staff must now provide additional information or options before Council will consider it again.
briefs.source_body:
7.4 LOCHDALE SCHOOL PARK PLAYGROUND

The Deputy General Manager Parks, Recreation and Culture submitted a report seeking Council direction on the future of the Lochdale School Park playground replacement.
Arising from discussion, the original recommendation was not moved and the following motion was introduced:

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 060

THAT the report titled “Lochdale School Park Playground”, dated February 10, 2026, be REFERRED back to staff.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $621,838 contract to Rollins Machinery for two refuse trucks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a contract award to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the supply and delivery of two split body refuse trucks. The total estimated cost is $621,838 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the unit prices submitted. The decision was carried unanimously.
briefs.source_body:
7.5 CA - TWO (2) SPLIT BODY REFUSE TRUCKS

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of two (2) Split Body Refuse Trucks.
*Councillor Gu left at 5:57 pm and returned at 6:00 pm

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 061
THAT a contract award to Rollins Machinery Ltd., for an estimated cost of $621,838 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – Two (2) Split Body Refuse Trucks”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on unit prices submitted.
CARRIED UNIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $648,206 contract to Rollins Machinery for refuse truck
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a contract award to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the supply and delivery of a Tandem Axle Front Load Refuse Truck. The total estimated cost is $648,206 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the unit prices submitted by the company. This decision is final and the contract has been awarded.
briefs.source_body:
7.6 CA - TANDEM AXLE FRONT LOAD REFUSE TRUCK

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of a Tandem Axle Front Load Refuse Truck.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 062
THAT a contract award to Rollins Machinery Ltd., for an estimated cost of $648,206 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – Tandem Axle Front Load Refuse Truck”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on unit prices submitted.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approved $20M fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved a $20 million contract award to 4Refuel Canada LP for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, bio-diesel and related fuel products. The contract was approved unanimously through Resolution No. 2026-063. Final payment will be based on actual quantities of fuel delivered and the unit prices quoted, not a fixed amount. This means the city has locked in a supplier for fuel products but will only pay for what it actually uses.
briefs.source_body:
7.7 CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FUEL PRODUCTS

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, bio-diesel and related fuel products.
MOVED AND SECONDED

Resolution No. 2026 - 063
THAT a contract award to 4Refuel Canada LP for an estimated cost of $20,000,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – Supply and Delivery of Fuel Products”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities of goods delivered and unit prices quoted.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $174,384 contract change for three Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks

(OpenRoad Toyota Richmond) for an estimated $174,384 including taxes. The final payment will be based on the unit prices submitted. The resolution was carried unanimously.

7.8 CC - THREE (3) TOYOTA TACOMA PICKUP TRUCKS

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for the supply and delivery of three (3) additional Toyota Tacoma Pickup Trucks.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 064
THAT a contract change to OpenRoad Auto Group Limited dba OpenRoad Toyota Richmond, for an estimated cost of $174,384 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CC – Three (3) Toyota Tacoma Pickup Trucks” dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and;
THAT final payment will be based on unit prices submitted.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $700,000 contract change for Campfire Archaeology consulting services

The change is estimated to cost $700,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantity of services delivered and the hourly rates submitted. This decision was made at the February 10, 2026 council meeting.

7.9 CC - ARCHAEOLOGICAL CONSULTING SERVICES

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for archaeological consulting services.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 065

THAT final payment will be based on actual quantity of services delivered and hourly rates as submitted.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $193,000 contract change for Comfort Keepers housekeeping services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a contract change for 0893967 B.C. Ltd., doing business as Comfort Keepers, to continue providing housekeeping services under the Better at Home (BHA) Program. The change is valued at an estimated $193,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual number of services delivered and the hourly rates submitted. This decision was made at the February 10, 2026 council meeting and is now final.
briefs.source_body:
7.10 CC - HOUSEKEEPING SERVICES BETTER AT HOME PROGRAM

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval to award a Contract Change (CC) for Housekeeping Services Better at Home (BHA) Program.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 066
THAT a contract change to 0893967 B.C. Ltd. DBA Comfort Keepers for an estimated cost of $193,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CC – Housekeeping Services Better at Home Program”, dated February 10, 2026 be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of services delivered and hourly rates as submitted.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $240,000 contract change for Astech hazardous material surveys
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a contract change for Astech Consultants Ltd. to continue providing hazardous building material survey services at various city locations. The change is for an estimated $240,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantity of services delivered and the unit prices proposed. This decision was made unanimously at the February 10, 2026 council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.11 CC - HAZARDOUS BUILDING MATERIAL SURVEY SERVICES

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for Pre-Project Hazardous Building Material Survey Services at various City Locations.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 067
THAT a contract change to Astech Consultants Ltd. for an estimated amount of $240,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CC - Hazardous Building Material Survey Services”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and,
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and unit prices proposed.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $7.3M one-year renewal of E-COMM police dispatch contract

The estimated cost is $7,300,000, with the final payment based on actual services delivered. This decision was made at the February 10, 2026 council meeting and is now in effect.
briefs.source_body:
7.12 CR - E-COMM POLICE DISPATCH SERVICES

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a one-year Contract Renewal (CR) for police dispatch services.

MOVED AND SECONDED

Resolution No. 2026 - 068
THAT a one-year contract renewal to E-COMM Emergency Communications for BC Incorporated for an estimated cost of $7,300,000, as outlined in the report titled “CR – E-COMM Police Dispatch Services”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on the actual services delivered.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council approved $267,000 fence rental contract renewal with Super Save Fence Rentals
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with Super Save Fence Rentals Inc. for the supply and delivery of fence rental services. The contract is valued at an estimated $267,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual quantities of goods and services delivered and the unit prices quoted. This decision was made at the February 10, 2026 council meeting.

7.13 CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FENCE RENTAL SERVICES

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the Supply and Delivery of Fence Rental Services.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 069
THAT a contract renewal to Super Save Fence Rentals Inc, for an estimated cost of $267,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Supply and Delivery of Fence Rental Services”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and

briefs.title: Council approved $380,000 road marking paint contract with Cloverdale Paint Inc
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a contract renewal with Cloverdale Paint Inc for the supply and delivery of road marking paint and glass beads. The contract is valued at an estimated $380,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on actual quantities delivered and the unit prices quoted. The resolution was carried unanimously.

The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the Supply and Delivery of Road Marking Paint & Glass Bead.

Resolution No. 2026 - 070
THAT a contract renewal to Cloverdale Paint Inc, for an estimated cost of $380,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Supply and Delivery of Road Marking Paint and Glass Bead”, dated February 10, 2026, be approved; and

briefs.title: Council approved up to $10,000 for PRCC members to attend BCRPA symposium
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved spending up to $10,000 for up to six members of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee (PRCC) to attend the 2026 BC Recreation and Parks Association (BCRPA) Symposium in Vancouver from April 28 to May 1, 2026. The decision was made unanimously at the council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
8.1 PRCC - 2026 BCRPA SYMPOSIUM

The Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee (PRCC) submitted a report seeking Council approval of an expenditure for the PRCC members to attend the BC Recreation and Parks Association (BCRPA) Symposium.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 071
THAT an expenditure of up to $10,000 for up to six (6) Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee members to attend the 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver, BC from April 28 - May 1, 2026, as discussed at the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee meeting on January 28, 2026, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council adopted the Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given final approval to the Consolidated Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw 2026 (Bylaw No. 14793). This bylaw sets out the city's spending and revenue plans for the next five years. The vote was carried, with Councillor Lee opposed. The plan is now in effect and will guide city budgets and tax rates through 2030.
briefs.source_body:
11.1 FINAL ADOPTION
11.1.1 #14793 - Burnaby Consolidated Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw 2026
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 076
THAT Bylaw No. 14793 be now read a final time and adopted.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Councillor Lee)

briefs.title: Council received petition against supportive housing at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to receive a petition from the Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch regarding proposed supportive/complex care housing at 5389 Imperial Street. The petition was received for information only — no decision was made on the housing project itself. Council simply acknowledged receipt of the petition and filed it.
briefs.source_body:
12.1 PETITION: Supportive / Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street (Supplementary)

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report providing Council a petition from the Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch regarding the proposed Supportive/Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 077
THAT the attached Supplementary Petition from the Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch regarding the proposed Supportive / Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street, Burnaby, received by Council on February 10, 2026, be received for information.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council referred Metro 2050 amendment options to staff for report
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to refer a Metro Vancouver request about proposed Metro 2050 amendment options to staff for a report. The request came from South of the Fraser Mayors. Council received the item and sent it to staff to study and report back. No changes have been made yet — this is a first step to gather more information before any future decision.
briefs.source_body:
12.2 Consultation on Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options in Response to South of the Fraser Mayors’ Request

Councillor Dhaliwal referred to Item 3.4. the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions package, dated February 10, 2026, from Metro Vancouver regarding consultation on proposed Metro 2050 amendment options in response to South of the Fraser Mayors’ request.

Arising from discussion, the following motion was introduced:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 078
THAT Item 3.4 “Metro Vancouver - Consultation on Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options in Response to South of the Fraser Mayors’ Request” of the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submission package, dated February 10, 2026 be REFERRED to staff for report.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Council tables motion opposing complex care housing at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted to table (set aside without a final decision) a motion that would have committed the city to oppose BC Housing's plan to build supportive and complex care housing at 5389 Imperial Street. The motion was tabled, meaning it is not approved or rejected — it is simply put aside for now. Councillors Lee and Wang voted against tabling.
briefs.source_body:
13.1 PROPOSED MOTION: Councillor Lee - BC Housing Complex Care at 5389 Imperial Street

Councillor Lee introduced a Proposed Motion seeking Council's commitment to not support Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street:
MOVED AND SECONDED

WHEREAS BC Housing has indicated its intention to develop Supportive Housing and Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street; and

WHEREAS the proposed location has generated significant and sustained community concern and opposition;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT Council commits that it will not support, approve, or endorse the development of Supportive Housing or Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street.
TABLED

Arising from discussion, the following motion was introduced:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 – 079
THAT Item 13.1 “Proposed Motion: Councillor Lee - BC Housing Complex Care at 5389 Imperial Street”, dated February 10, 2026, be now TABLED.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Councillors Lee and Wang)

briefs.title: Council approved donation campaign for tree canopy at Central Park and Halifax Park
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a donation and community matching campaign to increase tree canopy cover and beautify Central Park and Halifax Park. Staff will now move forward with setting up the campaign. This decision is final and the campaign is moving ahead.
briefs.source_body:
14. RELEASE OF CLOSED MEETING DECISIONS
Resolution No. C2025 - 119
THAT staff be directed to advance a proposed donation and community matching donation campaign for the purposes of enhancing canopy cover and beautification at Central Park and Halifax Park.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
